reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify Washington counties whose low-income student enrollment percentage was between 32% and 40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22.
2. From the counties retained in step 1, keep counties whose district-average math proficiency was above 40% in the 2021-22 school year.
3. Exclude the county that was the most populous in Washington according to the 2020 census.
4. For the remaining counties, compare their 2020 census populations and return the county with the lowest population in `X County` format.
original_final_question: Which Washington county had the lowest 2020 census population among those counties that (1) had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22, (2) had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in the 2021-22 school year, and (3) were not the most populous county in Washington? Only report the county name in the format of `X County`. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=4, Washington Report Card Enrollment 2018-22 - nodes 1-4):
Node 1: 2018-19 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 2: 2019-20 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 3: 2020-21 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Thurston, Whitman}
Node 4: 2021-22 Low-Income 32-40% -> {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
HOP 2 (d=2, Assessment data + King Wikipedia - nodes 5-6):
Node 5 (Assessment 2021-22): Math proficiency >40% -> {King, Kitsap, Whitman} (eliminates San Juan at 35.2%)
Node 6 (King Wikipedia): King is most populous -> NOT most populous = {Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
Node 8 (Whitman Wikipedia): 2020 population = 47,973; county seat is Colfax
Result: Whitman County, county seat = Colfax
